title: Minaria micromeria
taxonomy: Minaria micromeria is classified as a genus of flowering plants in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This genus is native to the Americas and is characterized by its small, blue flowers. Within the Apocynaceae family, Minaria micromeria belongs to the subfamily Rauvolfioideae, which comprises plants with similar characteristics and growth habits. Further classification of Minaria micromeria is as follows: it is placed in the tribe Tabernaemontaneae, which includes other flowering plants with distinct features. Minaria micromeria is a relatively new discovery and its classification may be subject to revision as more research becomes available.

intro: Minaria micromeria, commonly known as 'Pitomba', is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, which may aid in relieving symptoms of gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Minaria micromeria include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Minaria micromeria is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments for skin care.
